title: Stop Will In The Name Of Love

jokes.text: Everybody gets stood up once. - Once a month?
jokes.setup: Will tries to console Carlton about being stood up by saying everyone gets stood up once
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals it happens to him monthly, showing his romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy. Carlton's pathetic dating life is well-established character trait

jokes.text: Look, chances are, a girl who matches up with you... has already taken her own life.
jokes.setup: Will appears to console Carlton about computer dating
jokes.punchline: Brutal dark twist that any woman compatible with Carlton would be suicidal
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly crafted roast. Classic Will Smith delivery style with surprising darkness

jokes.text: I'll be back in the stockroom in the dark.
jokes.setup: Carlton responds to Will's harsh joke about his dating prospects
jokes.punchline: Carlton retreating to literal darkness matches his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Carlton's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Carlton. - No.
jokes.setup: Will presumably tries to get Carlton to come out
jokes.punchline: Carlton's flat refusal shows his complete dejection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing Carlton's mood

jokes.text: What took you so long, girl? Damn.
jokes.setup: A woman arrives looking for Carlton after he's been waiting hours
jokes.punchline: Will assumes she's late for Carlton's date and responds inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Will's casual assumption and inappropriate greeting fits his character

jokes.text: Once the bread is in those little slots, you just slide the lever down?
jokes.setup: Hilary is supposedly learning to cook
jokes.punchline: She's amazed by the basic concept of a toaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - wealthy socialite clueless about basic appliances

jokes.text: Not now, I'm learning how to cook.
jokes.setup: Someone interrupts Hilary
jokes.punchline: She's too busy 'learning to cook' (operating a toaster) to be bothered
jokes.explanation: Hilary's self-importance about the simplest tasks is character-perfect

jokes.text: Gee, Uncle Phil, how are you gonna top last year?
jokes.setup: Phil mentions doing the same thing as last year but better
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically asks how he'll improve on presumably bad plans
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for revealing Phil's unromantic Valentine's plans

jokes.text: Valentines fun for you and your lover... come sing at the Peacock for a 10 dollar cover.
jokes.setup: Carlton reveals his Valentine's Day karaoke event
jokes.punchline: The awkward rhyming flyer reveals how uncool his event is
jokes.explanation: Carlton's earnest attempt at romance is endearingly awkward

jokes.text: Gosh, for real? Aunt Viv, can I be excused from the table? I'm just too darned excited to eat.
jokes.setup: Carlton enthusiastically describes his karaoke plans
jokes.punchline: Will's mock excitement about the obviously lame event
jokes.explanation: Will's sarcastic over-enthusiasm perfectly mocks Carlton's earnestness

jokes.text: But Mom, Dad, I've reserved special seats right next to the speakers for you.
jokes.setup: Parents decline Carlton's karaoke invitation
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks seats next to speakers are a special treat, not torture
jokes.explanation: Carlton's obliviousness to what makes a good experience is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You big romantic guy you.
jokes.setup: Phil mentions having special plans
jokes.punchline: Vivian's sarcastic response suggests his plans aren't romantic
jokes.explanation: Standard marital sarcasm about unromantic husband

jokes.text: This is going to be a breeze.
jokes.setup: Hilary is preparing Valentine's dinner
jokes.punchline: Her confidence is ironic given her complete cooking incompetence
jokes.explanation: Sets up Hilary's cooking disaster - her confidence is comedically misplaced

jokes.text: Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Will offers to drive Ashley and her date
jokes.punchline: Ashley's immediate dread at the prospect
jokes.explanation: Ashley knows Will will be just as bad as her father

jokes.text: This is not going to help my reputation.
jokes.setup: Ashley realizes her father will drive her on her date
jokes.punchline: She understands how uncool this makes her look
jokes.explanation: Ashley's teenage concern about reputation is relatable

jokes.text: Boys will not even come near me... at school without having a lawyer present.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains how Phil's overprotectiveness affects her dating
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated claim that boys need legal representation to approach her
jokes.explanation: Clever exaggeration of Phil's intimidation tactics with good visual imagery

jokes.text: Yes.
jokes.setup: Ashley describes how Phil scares away boys
jokes.punchline: Phil's satisfied confirmation that this is exactly what he wants
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Phil is proud of intimidating teenage boys. Great economy.

jokes.text: Will, the last time daddy drove me and my date to the movies... he wound up sitting in the two seats behind us.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains why she doesn't want Phil to drive
jokes.punchline: Reveals Phil literally chaperoned by sitting behind them in theater
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Phil's extreme overprotectiveness

jokes.text: See, this girl that I'm dating tonight is kind of like a 6.6 on the Richter scale... so I wanna be there, you know, for all the aftershocks.
jokes.setup: Phil explains why he can't just drop them off
jokes.punchline: Uses earthquake metaphor for his attractive date and sexual innuendo about aftershocks
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor with sexual innuendo. Phil's smooth delivery contrasts with his usual stuffiness

jokes.text: Yeah, I know what you're saying. I'm doomed.
jokes.setup: Phil explains his romantic plans conflict with chaperoning
jokes.punchline: Ashley realizes she's trapped between two overprotective males
jokes.explanation: Ashley's resigned acceptance of her fate is relatable

jokes.text: Free Willy. Beethoven's 2nd? The Return of the Care Bears? - Oh, that was my first choice.
jokes.setup: Phil shows Ashley approved movies for her date
jokes.punchline: List gets progressively more childish, Ashley sarcastically picks the worst
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of increasingly inappropriate movie choices for a teenager's date

jokes.text: Oh, but if your mother asks tomorrow... I didn't get any extra butter on my popcorn.
jokes.setup: Phil is being overprotective about Ashley's date
jokes.punchline: He asks Ashley to lie about his own unhealthy eating
jokes.explanation: Phil's hypocrisy - strict with Ashley but asks her to cover his dietary cheating

jokes.text: I'm not gonna have my daughter involved in one of your wild evenings.
jokes.setup: Will offers to take Ashley on his date
jokes.punchline: Phil assumes Will's dates are wild and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Sets up for Will to reveal his surprisingly innocent plans

jokes.text: We playing miniature golf.
jokes.setup: Phil expects Will's date to be wild
jokes.punchline: Will reveals incredibly tame miniature golf plan
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - subverts expectation of Will's wild lifestyle

jokes.text: That'll be cool unless the church group shut that place down, you know?
jokes.setup: Will reveals his innocent miniature golf date
jokes.punchline: Even more innocent detail about church groups potentially being there
jokes.explanation: Escalates the wholesome nature of Will's plans

jokes.text: Well, I was thinking... we'd run some guns to Nicaragua... but we'll probably just come home.
jokes.setup: Phil asks what they'll do after miniature golf
jokes.punchline: Will jokes about gun running then deflates to coming straight home
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurd extreme then immediate deflation to boring reality

jokes.text: But if anything happens to her, those hands will be without fingers.
jokes.setup: Phil agrees to let Will take Ashley
jokes.punchline: Creative threat about removing Will's fingers if Ashley gets hurt
jokes.explanation: Creative threat that's specific and memorable, perfectly fits Phil's protective nature

jokes.text: Yes, sir. Lose my fingers, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil threatens to remove Will's fingers
jokes.punchline: Will's submissive repetition shows he's genuinely intimidated
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the threat, shows Phil actually intimidates Will

jokes.text: Now I know, most of you are wondering two things: 'How does he dress so well?'... and 'What is this thing called karaoke anyway?'
jokes.setup: Carlton hosts his karaoke Valentine's event
jokes.punchline: His delusional belief people wonder about his fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton delusion - he thinks people admire his preppy style

jokes.text: Actually, karaoke has two definitions. Carry, as in carry a tune... and oke, as in okey-dokey, let the good times roll.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains what karaoke means
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong etymology that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Carlton's confident but completely wrong explanation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Philip, why don't you be a man... and admit that you forgot Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Phil claims he made restaurant reservations
jokes.punchline: Vivian cuts through his excuse and calls him out directly
jokes.explanation: Direct marital confrontation - Vivian doesn't buy Phil's excuses

jokes.text: All right. I admit, I just forgot. How come you don't forget Thanksgiving?
jokes.setup: Vivian demands Phil admit he forgot Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Phil admits it but deflects by mentioning remembering Thanksgiving
jokes.explanation: Phil's weak attempt to deflect by citing food-related holiday he remembers

jokes.text: Look who just floated in on cloud nine, Philip and Vivian Banks... two people, who, over the years, never stopped loving each other. I should know, my room was just down the hall.
jokes.setup: Carlton introduces his parents at the karaoke event
jokes.punchline: Awkward implication that he could hear them having sex
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton overshare - too innocent to realize how inappropriate his comment is

jokes.text: Let's get out of here. - Just...
jokes.setup: Carlton makes inappropriate comment about hearing his parents
jokes.punchline: Phil and Vivian's mortified desire to escape immediately
jokes.explanation: Natural parental reaction to son's mortifying overshare

jokes.text: Wait a minute, it looks as though she's getting some help from her caddy.
jokes.setup: Will does golf commentary at mini golf
jokes.punchline: He positions himself as her 'caddy' to get close physically
jokes.explanation: Will's smooth move disguised as sports commentary

jokes.text: Will, I thought you were the announcer. Hush up, I'm trying to help you with your form.
jokes.setup: Samantha questions Will's dual role
jokes.punchline: Will drops the announcer pretense to focus on getting close to her
jokes.explanation: Will abandons his elaborate setup for direct flirtation

jokes.text: Stop, not in front of your cousin. - Oh, yeah, yeah, you're right.
jokes.setup: Will is getting physically close while 'helping' with golf form
jokes.punchline: Samantha has to tell him to behave in front of Ashley
jokes.explanation: Implies Will was getting inappropriate during golf 'instruction'

jokes.text: Come on, let's go out to the car. - Stop.
jokes.setup: Will realizes he needs privacy
jokes.punchline: His immediate suggestion to go to the car for obvious reasons
jokes.explanation: Will's predictable teenage boy move to get alone time

jokes.text: Probably the same thing you were saying to girls when you were his age.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks what Will is saying to his date
jokes.punchline: Bryan deflects by referencing Ashley's past romantic experience
jokes.explanation: Bryan tries to seem worldly by implying Ashley has romantic experience

jokes.text: I doubt it. When I was his age, I was trying to get the girls to...
jokes.setup: Bryan starts to explain what he was trying to do with girls
jokes.punchline: He cuts himself off before saying something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat where Bryan almost reveals too much about his intentions

jokes.text: No, no. He's just a little kid and so is Ashley.
jokes.setup: Bryan was about to say something sexual about his past
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly tries to cover by calling both Will and Ashley little kids
jokes.explanation: Bryan's awkward backpedal after nearly revealing sexual thoughts

jokes.text: Not so little, Will. She is a beautiful girl. Didn't you see what the putter guy did? - No, what did he do? - He dropped her ball on purpose.
jokes.setup: Bryan points out Ashley is attractive and mentions the putter guy
jokes.punchline: Reveals the employee dropped her ball to check her out
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's growing awareness that Ashley is attracting male attention

jokes.text: Now, why would he do that, unless he was trying to check out her...?
jokes.setup: Bryan explains why the employee dropped Ashley's ball
jokes.punchline: He starts to say something crude but cuts himself off
jokes.explanation: Bryan almost says something inappropriate about Ashley's body

jokes.text: No, look, that is not possible.
jokes.setup: Bryan implies men are checking out Ashley sexually
jokes.punchline: Will's complete denial of the obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Will's protective denial mirrors Phil's earlier behavior

jokes.text: Hey, when I was 15, it was possible. Yeah, but you was a little freak though.
jokes.setup: Bryan says at 15 he was interested in sex
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses him as abnormally perverted
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt to dismiss normal teenage sexuality as abnormal

jokes.text: No, I'm just saying things were a lot faster in them public high schools.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain away Bryan's teenage sexuality
jokes.punchline: Blames public school environment for sexual awareness
jokes.explanation: Will's class-based explanation for sexual behavior

jokes.text: She goes to Bel-Air Academy. I went to St. John's Parochial.
jokes.setup: Will distinguishes between public and private school sexual awareness
jokes.punchline: Both Ashley and Bryan went to elite private schools
jokes.explanation: Undermines Will's theory about public vs private school sexuality

jokes.text: You still got that little dress and them shoes?
jokes.setup: Bryan mentions attending Catholic parochial school
jokes.punchline: Will jokes about Catholic school uniform with dress-like appearance
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke about stereotypical Catholic school uniforms

jokes.text: Hey, Carl Lewis, put a move on it. Hey, take it easy, Pops, you'll be in a hole soon enough.
jokes.setup: Will calls Bryan 'Carl Lewis' (sprinter) to hurry up
jokes.punchline: Bryan's comeback about Will being in a 'hole' (golf pun/sexual innuendo)
jokes.explanation: Clever double pun - golf hole and sexual innuendo, good comeback structure

jokes.text: That's a really nice outfit.
jokes.setup: Bryan compliments Ashley's outfit
jokes.punchline: This innocent compliment will trigger Will's overprotective response
jokes.explanation: Setup for Will's overreaction to innocent teenage flirtation

jokes.text: Hey, look, if I make this putt, you got to promise me that you'll... Oh, Will.
jokes.setup: Bryan tries to make a romantic wager with Ashley
jokes.punchline: Ashley cuts him off when she sees Will approaching protectively
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Will's intervention in innocent teenage romance

jokes.text: But there's really nothing to it.
jokes.setup: Hilary was worried about cooking dinner herself
jokes.punchline: Her confident declaration before disaster strikes
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for impending cooking disaster - overconfidence before the fall

jokes.text: Oh, Miss Hilary, could you pass me that heavy iron skillet over there? Hello. How thoughtless of me.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks Hilary to pass him a heavy skillet
jokes.punchline: Hilary presumably drops it or handles it incorrectly, injuring Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup - Hilary's clumsiness with kitchen equipment

jokes.text: No. Can you say that to me? Well, let's try it again. No. By George, I've got it. - No, no.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey tries to leave to tend to baby Nicky
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey repeatedly says 'No' to Hilary's demands, finally standing up to her
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Geoffrey finally asserting himself against Hilary's demands

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks for a do-over of Geoffrey's refusal
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey repeats his refusal even more firmly
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey doubles down on his refusal. The repetition creates a running gag while showing his determination to escape Hilary's chaos.

jokes.text: Good God.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey returns to see Hilary's cooking results
jokes.punchline: His horrified reaction to the kitchen disaster
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's understated reaction to Hilary's cooking catastrophe

jokes.text: I think there's something wrong with the stove.
jokes.setup: Kitchen is presumably destroyed from Hilary's cooking attempt
jokes.punchline: Hilary blames the equipment rather than her incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response - never takes responsibility for her failures

jokes.text: Hole in one, yes! My first one ever.
jokes.setup: Ashley achieves her first hole-in-one at miniature golf
jokes.punchline: Her excitement about this minor accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Sets up for Will's misunderstanding and bird dropping joke

jokes.text: Hole in one, yes! My first one ever.
jokes.setup: Ashley is celebrating a golf achievement
jokes.punchline: Her excitement over mini golf success shows her innocence
jokes.explanation: Ashley's genuine excitement about mini golf contrasts with the adults' sexual tension. Shows her remaining childlike enthusiasm.

jokes.text: That bird just doodied on your head. Will, I got a hole in one. Oh, you'll do better next time, baby.
jokes.setup: Bryan points out bird droppings, Ashley repeats her achievement
jokes.punchline: Will misses her success, thinks she's upset about a bad shot
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Will's patronizing response to what he thinks is failure

jokes.text: I got a idea. Hey, how about we switch partners. You go down there and play with... - Samantha. - Thanks a lot, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Will suggests switching golf partners
jokes.punchline: Ashley sarcastically helps him remember his own date's name
jokes.explanation: Ashley's sarcastic assistance highlights Will's neglect of his own date

jokes.text: Oh, you got another hole in one, baby? You good.
jokes.setup: Will continues to misunderstand Ashley's golfing success
jokes.punchline: His condescending praise for what he thinks are bad shots
jokes.explanation: Will's continued patronizing tone while missing Ashley's actual success

jokes.text: Gosh, it's getting warm out here. - Why don't you take your jacket off?
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions being warm
jokes.punchline: Bryan's suggestion that will trigger Will's overprotective response
jokes.explanation: Setup for Will to overreact to innocent suggestion about removing jacket

jokes.text: See... It's some killer bees coming from Mexico. And they could strike at any time. And they love teenage flesh.
jokes.setup: Will tries to prevent Ashley from removing her jacket
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous story about Mexican killer bees targeting teenage flesh
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd excuse showing Will's desperation to keep Ashley covered up

jokes.text: Thanks, but I'll risk it.
jokes.setup: Will warns about killer bees targeting teenage flesh
jokes.punchline: Ashley's dry response shows she's not buying his ridiculous story
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Will's absurd overprotectiveness

jokes.text: Just don't worry, man, I ain't gonna let no bees get to this honey.
jokes.setup: Will continues his killer bee story
jokes.punchline: Calls Ashley 'honey' in context of protecting her from bees
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting 'honey' endearment with bee protection story

jokes.text: Okay, Junior Mac, listen. I think, it's time we had a little chat, mano a boyo.
jokes.setup: Will decides to confront Bryan directly
jokes.punchline: 'Mano a boyo' - man to boy in Spanish, emphasizing age difference
jokes.explanation: Clever Spanish wordplay emphasizing Will's condescending view of Bryan

jokes.text: Will, what are you doing?
jokes.setup: Will is confronting Bryan aggressively
jokes.punchline: Ashley's confused question about his obvious interference
jokes.explanation: Ashley's reasonable question about Will's increasingly obvious protective behavior. Sets up the confrontation about his interference.

jokes.text: I think, the problem is something called a double standard.
jokes.setup: Bryan responds to Will's confrontation
jokes.punchline: Astute observation about Will's hypocrisy regarding teenage behavior
jokes.explanation: Bryan intelligently calls out Will's hypocritical behavior

jokes.text: I know that routine... I invented that routine.
jokes.setup: Will accuses Bryan of using an innocent act
jokes.punchline: Will admits he created the same manipulative routine
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment where Will admits his own past manipulative behavior

jokes.text: I see right through you, you little dog.
jokes.setup: Will continues his confrontation with Bryan
jokes.punchline: Calls Bryan a 'little dog' - implies he's a player/predator
jokes.explanation: Will's street slang insult showing his protective aggression

jokes.text: Fun? Does this look like a place to have fun? I don't think so. Ain't nobody gonna have no fun up in here. Not me, not you, not her... - And definitely not me.
jokes.setup: Bryan mentions wanting to have fun
jokes.punchline: Will's escalating declaration that no one will have fun, Samantha agrees
jokes.explanation: Will's authoritarian declaration ruins everyone's evening, Samantha's addition emphasizes the impact

jokes.text: Goodbye, Will. Whoa, baby, come back here... What is her name?
jokes.setup: Samantha leaves after Will ruins the date
jokes.punchline: Will still doesn't remember her name even as she's leaving
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Will's continued neglect of his own date's name

jokes.text: The word was out on your father... but who knew your whole family was crazy?
jokes.setup: Bryan is leaving after Will's confrontation
jokes.punchline: Reveals Phil's reputation for craziness extends to family knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phil's established reputation, escalating to include Will

jokes.text: Hey, let him go. Let him go, baby. And good riddance, you little Filthy McNasty.
jokes.setup: Ashley tries to stop Bryan from leaving
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous nickname 'Filthy McNasty' for Bryan
jokes.explanation: 'Filthy McNasty' is an absurd and memorable insult that fits Will's over-the-top reaction

jokes.text: Will, how could you do this to me? To you? Ashley, I'm doing this for you.
jokes.setup: Ashley is upset about her ruined date
jokes.punchline: Will's self-righteous claim he's helping her
jokes.explanation: Will's delusional belief that ruining Ashley's date was helpful

jokes.text: Look, let me make it better. I'm gonna buy you some ice cream. No, I don't want anything. You're worse than daddy.
jokes.setup: Will tries to console Ashley with ice cream
jokes.punchline: Ashley compares Will negatively to her already overprotective father
jokes.explanation: Strong callback - Will has become even worse than Phil at overprotection

jokes.text: Nice job, Cupid.
jokes.setup: Will has completely ruined Valentine's Day romance
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comparison to Cupid, god of love
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic nickname - Will destroyed love instead of promoting it

jokes.text: That's what I call a happy Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Will has ruined everyone's Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic assessment of the disaster he created
jokes.explanation: Will's sarcastic recognition of the Valentine's Day disaster

jokes.text: Yeah, right, like you can handle some big old dude trying to be all over you... like cheese on a Big Mac.
jokes.setup: Ashley claims she can handle boys' advances
jokes.punchline: Will's vivid metaphor comparing male attention to cheese on a Big Mac
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor that's both funny and reveals Will's crude thinking about romance

jokes.text: Will, maybe I like cheese.
jokes.setup: Will compares male attention to cheese on a Big Mac
jokes.punchline: Ashley continues the metaphor to suggest she likes male attention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that subverts Will's metaphor - Ashley owns her sexuality

jokes.text: How you gonna like something you ain't never had? Please tell me you ain't never had no cheese, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley says she might like 'cheese' (sexual experience)
jokes.punchline: Will's panicked realization of what the metaphor means
jokes.explanation: Will's horror at realizing his own metaphor's implications about Ashley's experience

jokes.text: It's cheese, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley speaks directly about sex
jokes.punchline: Will desperately tries to return to the safer metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan attempt to retreat from serious conversation back to silly metaphor

jokes.text: Maybe we need to send you to an all-girls school, you know. Just so you keep your head on your studies.
jokes.setup: Will realizes Ashley is growing up sexually
jokes.punchline: His extreme solution of all-girls school to prevent romance
jokes.explanation: Will's overprotective solution mirrors conservative parental fears

jokes.text: I took it out when I put in the hot tub.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if he has spare stove in his truck
jokes.punchline: Repair guy has hot tub instead of spare parts in work truck
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities - repair guy values recreation over professional equipment

jokes.text: Or wasn't there a sign or something in the trailer park?
jokes.setup: Hilary is frustrated repair can't be done on Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Her classist assumption about repair guy living in trailer park
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary classism - assumes working-class person lives in trailer park

jokes.text: But while I'm here, maybe I can adjust your attitude.
jokes.setup: Hilary makes classist comment about trailer park
jokes.punchline: Duane's smooth threat disguised as repair terminology
jokes.explanation: Great comeback using repair metaphor to threaten attitude adjustment

jokes.text: Some old lady fractured her hip and broke her leg in five places. Do I have the worst luck or what?
jokes.setup: Hilary's date cancelled due to medical emergency
jokes.punchline: She focuses on her bad luck rather than patient's suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary selfishness - makes someone else's injury about her inconvenience

jokes.text: I was supposed to go out clubbing with the Maytag repairman. He don't get out much, you know.
jokes.setup: Duane mentions his cancelled plans
jokes.punchline: Reference to lonely Maytag repairman advertising campaign
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to famous commercial about lonely appliance repairman

jokes.text: I'm too good for you.
jokes.setup: Duane asks Hilary on impromptu Valentine's date
jokes.punchline: Hilary's brutally direct class-based rejection
jokes.explanation: Peak Hilary - brutally honest about her snobbishness with perfect economy

jokes.text: I usually don't say this to a woman, but you know what? You're probably right. Happy Valentine's Day, pretty lady.
jokes.setup: Hilary brutally rejects Duane as beneath her
jokes.punchline: Duane graciously agrees with her assessment and remains polite
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly gracious response to brutal rejection - maintains dignity while acknowledging truth

jokes.text: Duane? Do you fix microwaves?
jokes.setup: Hilary rejected Duane as too low-class for her
jokes.punchline: She immediately needs his professional services again
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - Hilary's need for working-class skills immediately after class-based rejection

jokes.text: Stop it, Vivian. You don't wanna be here any more than I do.
jokes.setup: Vivian is trying to enjoy Carlton's karaoke event
jokes.punchline: Phil calls out their mutual misery at son's terrible party
jokes.explanation: Phil's honest assessment of their shared suffering at Carlton's event

jokes.text: I work very hard... to provide a good life for you and my family. And every night, I hold you in my arms, I kiss you goodnight. I don't need a holiday to tell you that I love you. I show you all year.
jokes.setup: Phil and Vivian argue about Valentine's Day neglect
jokes.punchline: Phil's unexpectedly romantic speech about showing love daily
jokes.explanation: Phil's romantic redemption moment - shows depth beneath his usual stuffiness

jokes.text: We're going to Paris on our anniversary. Philip. Oh, that's next week. It is?
jokes.setup: Phil mentions Paris trip for their anniversary
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know when their anniversary actually is
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Phil's romantic failures - he planned Paris but doesn't know when

jokes.text: I just paid a 10 dollar cover to spend Valentine's Day with my parents.
jokes.setup: Ashley arrives at Carlton's karaoke event alone
jokes.punchline: She paid money to spend Valentine's with parents instead of on date
jokes.explanation: Good callback to cover charge - Ashley's romantic evening became family obligation

jokes.text: You took a bus?
jokes.setup: Ashley explains how she got to the karaoke bar
jokes.punchline: Phil's horror at his daughter using public transportation
jokes.explanation: Phil's upper-class shock at daughter using public transit

jokes.text: I did. That's why I kicked that old nasty boyfriend's butt out.
jokes.setup: Phil accuses Will of not watching Ashley properly
jokes.punchline: Will claims kicking out Bryan was protective behavior
jokes.explanation: Will's delusional justification of his overprotective behavior

jokes.text: Hey, a little less yelling and shouting, a little more humming and clapping.
jokes.setup: Family argument breaks out during Carlton's Valentine's event
jokes.punchline: Carlton tries to maintain party atmosphere despite family drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness - tries to maintain karaoke mood during family crisis

jokes.text: We was at the Putt Putt Golf and she was necking.
jokes.setup: Will explains what happened on the double date
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated description of Ashley's innocent behavior
jokes.explanation: Will's exaggerated retelling makes innocent teenage behavior sound scandalous
